pattern: Lay the linked-list values into a grid by walking the classic shrinking-boundary spiral, consuming one node per cell and padding any cells left over with a sentinel.
hints: Pre-fill the whole grid with the placeholder, then overwrite cells in spiral order only while nodes remain.
Track four moving walls — top, bottom, left, right — and traverse one edge at a time, tightening the relevant wall after each pass.
Stop early the moment the list runs out; remaining cells keep their placeholder value.

edge_inputs.description: single row, list shorter than the row
single column fills top to bottom
grid larger than the list so the interior is padded
square grid fully consumed with no padding
list exactly fills a wide rectangle

stress.description: random dimensions with a list length up to the cell count, sometimes short to force padding
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = max(2, min(size, 30))
    m = rng.randint(1, cap)
    n = rng.randint(1, cap)
    total = m * n
    length = rng.randint(1, total)
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(length)]
    return [m, n, vals]
stress.note: 1 <= length <= m*n keeps the input valid

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def spiralMatrix(self, m, n, head):
        grid = [[-1] * n for _ in range(m)]
        top, bottom, left, right = 0, m - 1, 0, n - 1
        node = head
        while node and top <= bottom and left <= right:
            c = left
            while node and c <= right:
                grid[top][c] = node.val
                node = node.next
                c += 1
            top += 1
            r = top
            while node and r <= bottom:
                grid[r][right] = node.val
                node = node.next
                r += 1
            right -= 1
            if top <= bottom:
                c = right
                while node and c >= left:
                    grid[bottom][c] = node.val
                    node = node.next
                    c -= 1
                bottom -= 1
            if left <= right:
                r = bottom
                while node and r >= top:
                    grid[r][left] = node.val
                    node = node.next
                    r -= 1
                left += 1
        return grid
